rationale: Both correctly reject the vendor-pricing story, preserve the regulator/inspection-allocation/system-harm invariants, and bound unsupported pricing and profit interpretations. A is closer to the requested full-audit contract: it exposes readiness, DFC-12, all gates, a mismatch map, explicit repairs, layer audits, and boundaries. However, A assigns Stage 2 despite missing constraints, timing, information, uncertainty, and mechanism, and its proposed narrative invents a capacity trade-off in which attention to one target necessarily reduces attention elsewhere. That is a material model addition under a request allowing only model-preserving repairs. B correctly assigns Stage 1 and avoids specifying that scarcity mechanism, so fidelity controls the overall preference. B is also appreciably leaner and better organized, though it omits the complete DFC-12/full BusinessBrief record and still repeats the core three-way mismatch across several sections. Each output unnecessarily exposes scholarly positioning for a task that does not request it.
